rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CANTILLANO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance, And For An Accounting
rulings.Rulings: This demurrer is untimely, and the meet and confer process does not appear to comply with the requirements of CCP section 430.4, as neither the legal basis for the demurrer nor authority supporting that basis were presented in the meet and confer communications between counsel that have been presented to the Court. Nevertheless, Petitioner does not appear to have been prejudiced by these procedural defects, as he was able to file a substantive opposition to the demurrer. This demurrer is noticed solely on the basis of the petition's causes of action being time-barred under CCP section 366.2. The demurrer on this basis is overruled. Based on the face of the pleadings, the one-year statutory limitation of CCP section 366.2 is inapplicable. The causes of action are all alleged against Respondent, and not against the late settlor or any other decedent. However, despite the limited grounds stated in the notice for the order on demurrer, and the basic tenet of motion practice that the notice of motion must state the grounds for the order being sought and the court generally may only consider the grounds stated in the notice of motion, the parties engaged in debating other potential statutes of limitations in the papers, without complaint or opposition from Petitioner on the expansion of the demurrer beyond the noticed basis. Insofar as the papers may be read as further demur on the basis of the statute of limitations under either CCP sections 342 or 338, it is overruled. On the face of the pleadings, the Petition alleges Petitioner discovered the facts underlying the Petition's causes of action after his father's death in November 2019. The Petition was filed fewer than four years after this alleged period of discovery. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 19, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
